SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper clearly states its main claim (INQ converts pretrained 32-bit CNNs to low-precision powers-of-two/zero with little or no accuracy loss) and supports it with extensive experiments on ImageNet across AlexNet, VGG-16, GoogleNet and ResNets (Section 3 and Table summaries). The mathematical parts are heuristic (quantizer in Eq.4 and the n1 formula (Eq.2) are plausible but not derived rigorously) and some notation/expressions (e.g. n2 computation) are ambiguous in the provided text. Experimental methodology is broadly sound (uses standard baselines, ablation of partition strategies in Section 3.2, and comparisons to prior work), but lacks statistical reporting (no multiple runs or significance tests) and mixes pretrained sources (ResNet-18) which can affect comparisons. Overall the evidence supports the claims empirically, with minor issues in formal derivation and statistical treatment.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper proposes a clearly different methodological approach: rather than globally quantizing all weights, INQ performs importance-aware weight partitioning, group-wise quantization to powers-of-two (with a variable-length encoding, Sec.2.1) and iterative re-training until all weights are quantized (Sec.2.2–2.3). This incremental, pruning-inspired quantize-then-retrain loop is not just an application of existing singular techniques (e.g., BinaryConnect, vector quantization, or Han et al.’s deep compression) but a non-trivial integration tailored to avoid accuracy loss. The paper also contrasts INQ explicitly with prior global-quantization methods and demonstrates it yields lossless/near-lossless results on large models, so the contribution is a notable and differentiated novelty rather than purely incremental.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (clear Intro → Method (with subsections) → Experiments → Conclusion and Appendices) and states its main contributions early (Intro lists the INQ idea and key empirical findings). Notation is mostly consistent and defined (Pl, n1/n2, Tl, A(1)/A(2), Algorithm 1), though there are minor slips (e.g., a small typo referencing A2 vs A(2)) and a few places where formulas rely on explained constants (4/3, 3/2) that could use slightly more justification. Figures/tables and the algorithm are properly referenced and appear to support the claims, but the provided text contains duplicated/garbled passages (especially in the appendix region) and some repetitive phrasing that hurt readability and introduce grammatical noise. Overall the presentation is clear with only minor editing issues.
